## Blue UAS Certified with High-Resolution Thermal Imaging Optimized for Nighttime Operations
Teal 2 is an American-made sUAS developed under strict design guidelines that has earned it a Blue UAS Certification by the U.S. Department of Defense. The Teal 2’s primary application is short-range reconnaissance missions. Combat soldiers, police officers, firefighters, wildlife managers, and industrial inspectors rely on the Teal 2 to achieve mission success.
Integrated FLIR imaging lets you see what is hidden to the naked eye.
Convert light or changes in light into electronic signals for full visibility.
Identify targets, track movement, and assess threats more easily.
Full range of thermal palettes lets you see what others can’t.
White Hot is useful when operating at night, as the screen is mostly dark and night vision is better preserved. 
White Hot is useful when operating at night, as the screen is mostly dark and night vision is better preserved. 
Black Hot displays body heat in a clear, stark image with a high level of detail, and is often used by law enforcement, military personnel, and search and rescue teams. 
Black Hot displays body heat in a clear, stark image with a high level of detail, and is often used by law enforcement, military personnel, and search and rescue teams. 
The Hottest isotherm / color palette is best used in high-contrast environmental conditions, as it instantly displays hot spots, such as fires, leaks, or body heat against a grayscale image.
The Hottest isotherm / color palette is best used in high-contrast environmental conditions, as it instantly displays hot spots, such as fires, leaks, or body heat against a grayscale image. 
The Hottest isotherm / color palette is best used in high-contrast environmental conditions, as it instantly displays hot spots, such as fires, leaks, or body heat against a grayscale image. 
The lighter color contrasted against the darker shading aids in quickly detecting heat sources, and is useful to show slight temperature changes. 
The lighter color contrasted against the darker shading aids in quickly detecting heat sources, and is useful to show slight temperature changes. 
The Ironbow color palette quickly identifies thermal anomalies by showing heat distribution and subtle details, and can be ideal in low contrast environments.

## GO ON A MISSION AND DEPLOY THE GAME-CHANGING CAPABILITIES OF TEAL 2's THERMAL HEAT SIGNATURES.
Equipped with the FLIR Hadron 640R EO/IR payload, optimized for nighttime operations.
Multi-vehicle command and control provides a 360-degree view of a target or ISR on multiple targets.
Compact and rugged design enables fast deployment in challenging environments
Teal 2 provides a high level of security through its full encryption using the AES-256 standard, ensuring that data and communication remain confidential and protected from unauthorized access.
  * Weight: 2.75 lbs (1.25 kg) 
  * Flight Speed: 10 m/s (23 mph) 
  * Max Flight Ceiling: 10,000 ft (3048 m) MSL 
  * Max Range (From Controller): 3.1 Miles (5 km) 
  * Operation Temp Range: -32 to 110 F (-35.6 to 43.3 C) 
  * Wind Limits: 18 mph (16 kn) Sustained to 25 mph (22 kn) Gusts 
  * Weight: 3.7 lbs (1.68 kg) 


  * System: Dual Axis EO/IR Gimbal with Hadron 640R 
  * EO Sensor: 16MP, 67 degree HFOV 
  * EO Video Recording: 4000x3000 at 15fps 
  * Gimbal Pitch Control Range: -120 to +120 
  * IR Sensor: Boson 640 Radiometric, 32 degree HFOV 
  * IR Video Recording: 640x512 at 30Hz 
  * Recorded Video Format: MP4 and Transport Stream 
  * Image Signal Processor: Spectra 280 


*US Standard Atmosphere 1976, which is 59F, Sea Level, 29.92 Barometric Pressure
